compiled_state.title: As a DSO user on a large tracker, I get fast ticket list responses because archived tickets are skipped via a marker-file check
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the .archived marker-file fast-skip in the ticket reducer. Every ARCHIVED event writer drops an .archived marker (after the event is durable, same git commit). reduce_all_tickets() checks for the marker via stat() before invoking reduce_ticket(), skipping event replay when the marker is present. A one-shot backfill script creates markers for the 13K existing archived tickets. A timing script under tests/perf/ demonstrates the improvement.

**Why**: This is the primary performance win of the epic. `ticket list` on the 16K-ticket tracker drops from 2.8s to near-instant once markers are in place, eliminating the 30-60s of accumulated wait per sprint session.

**Scope**:
- IN: .archived marker write path (ticket-lifecycle.sh + Jira sync path + any other ARCHIVED event writer identified by grep audit); marker remove path in ticket-revert.sh (under flock, atomic with commit); reduce_all_tickets() stat() check; one-shot backfill script (ticket-archive-markers-backfill.sh) with net-archival-state computation; before/after timing script under tests/perf/ with configurable --threshold (default ≥20%)
- OUT: Orphan-marker self-heal (deferred to S1b); compute_dir_hash() cache-key extension (deferred to S1b); CI integration of timing script (scoped as future follow-up per epic SC 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `reduce_all_tickets()` skips archived ticket directories via a stat() check for `.archived` before invoking `reduce_ticket()`; marker-absent + ARCHIVED event present → slow-path fallback with correct state
  ← Satisfies: "`reduce_all_tickets()` skips archived ticket directories via a filesystem `stat()` check for an `.archived` marker before invoking `reduce_ticket()`"
- When this story is complete, every ARCHIVED event writer invokes the marker-write path (confirmed by grep audit); marker is written after the event is durable and in the same git commit; failed marker write does not prevent event commit
  ← Satisfies: "Every ARCHIVED-event writer... invokes the marker-write path. The marker is written after the ARCHIVED event is durably on disk AND committed to git"
- When this story is complete, `ticket revert` removes the marker when reverting an ARCHIVED event, acquires the per-ticket `fcntl.flock`, commits marker removal atomically with the revert; rolls back on commit failure
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket revert` removes the `.archived` marker when reverting an ARCHIVED event, acquiring the same per-ticket `fcntl.flock`... committed atomically... rolled back on commit failure"
- When this story is complete, a backfill script `ticket-archive-markers-backfill.sh` (matching `ticket-compact-all.sh` pattern) creates markers idempotently for all existing tickets with archived net state; safe to re-run; acquires per-ticket flock
  ← Satisfies: "A one-shot backfill script... creates `.archived` markers idempotently... computes each ticket's net archival state by reducing its event log"
- When this story is complete, a timing script under `tests/perf/` emits PASS/FAIL against a configurable `--threshold` arg (default: ≥20% wall-clock reduction for `ticket list`); runnable standalone
  ← Satisfies: "A before/after timing script... emits PASS/FAIL against a configurable `--threshold` argument (default: ≥20% wall-clock reduction)"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Marker write ordering (event-before-marker) is correctness-critical; include a crash-injection test that asserts the orphan-marker state is recovered by S1b's self-heal OR falls to slow path (SC 1 ensures correctness even if S1b is not yet merged)
- [Performance] Backfill on 16K corpus uses per-ticket flock (not a global lock); serial execution acceptable — follows compact-all pattern
- [Maintainability] Modifies `ticket-lifecycle.sh`, `ticket-revert.sh`, `reduce_all_tickets()`; merge conflict risk shared with S2 — epic mandates merge order S1 → S2
- [Testing] Crash injection pattern: if not present in the repo, adopt an env-var gate (e.g., `DSO_FAULT_INJECT=between-event-and-marker`) that makes the marker-write a no-op; simple and reproducible
- [Security] No new auth/trust boundaries — N/A
- [Accessibility] No UI changes — N/A

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
